commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the useful legacy hardlink compatibility while fixing the target-existence check and adding missing corrupt-archive regressions.; Needs attention: The patch weakens a hardlink archive validation boundary by allowing suffix matches against Link entries.; Review finding: [P1] Exclude link entries from suffix target matches — src/commands/backup-verify.ts:307-308 (sha=eda64f61d5cda63fb013a0c5aa6f7f2c09831884)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `backup verify` to accept legacy hardlink linkpaths by exact or suffix entry match and adds regression tests for cwd-relative node-tar hardlink archives.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +90. Total +100 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: node-tar v7.5.15 writes hardlink linkpaths as cwd-relative paths, and current main rejects those when they are not under the manifest archive root. I did not run a local repro because this review kept the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the useful legacy hardlink compatibility while fixing the target-existence check and adding missing corrupt-archive regressions.

target.title: fix(backup): verify legacy internal hardlink targets
target.body:
## Summary
- `backup verify` rejected previously valid backup archives that contain internal hardlinks, failing with `Archive hardlink target is outside the declared archive root` (#89257).
- Root cause: node-tar records a deduplicated hardlink's `linkpath` as the **cwd-relative source path** (e.g. `.openclaw/state/db`), while `onWriteEntry` only remaps the entry's own `path` into the `<root>/payload/posix/...` namespace. `verifyHardlinkTargetsAgainstArchiveRoot` compared the cwd-relative linkpath against the archive root, so any genuine internal hardlink looked "outside" it.
- Fix: accept a hardlink target when it matches a real archive entry exactly **or by path suffix** (proving the linked file is present in the archive). Absolute-path, backslash, and `..` traversal rejection is unchanged — it still happens earlier in `normalizeArchivePath`, and `verifyManifestAgainstEntries` still enforces that every entry lives under the archive root.

This complements #89328, which targets the same issue but reconstructs the target via `path.posix.join(archiveRoot, linkpath)`. That only re-adds the root segment and assumes the legacy linkpath is `payload/posix/...`; node-tar never emits that shape, so a real `openclaw backup create` archive (linkpath `.openclaw/state/...`) still fails verification under that approach. Suffix matching here handles both the real cwd-relative shape and the root-relative shape.

Out of scope (documented in the issue): the `backup create --verify` exit-13 / truncated `.tmp` half is a separate failure (Node unsettled top-level await abandoning the tar write mid-stream) that needs live user state to reproduce, matching the existing triage note. Not addressed here.

Refs #89257

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/infra/backup-create.test.ts src/commands/backup.create-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.atomic.test.ts src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check` / `oxlint` / `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm check:test-types` — all clean
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** `backup verify` now accepts older archives whose internal hardlink targets are recorded as node-tar cwd-relative paths, while still rejecting absolute, backslash, traversal, and missing hardlink targets.

**Real environment tested:** Source checkout on this branch. Reproduced the verifier rejection against a genuine archive built with real `fs.link` + `tar.c` (default linkCache, so dedup is on and the link entry carries the actual cwd-relative `linkpath` of `.openclaw/state/a.bin`), then confirmed acceptance after the fix.

**Exact steps / commands run after this patch:**
- Built a real hardlinked archive (two `fs.link`ed files under a state dir, `cwd` ancestry forcing node-tar dedup, `onWriteEntry` remap via the production `buildBackupArchivePath`) and ran `backupVerifyCommand` on it.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts` → 12 passed.

**Evidence after fix:** Two new regression tests pass — one pins the exact legacy cwd-relative `linkpath` shape, and one builds a genuine deduplicated-hardlink archive end-to-end so the test can't false-green on a hand-encoded approximation. The existing unsafe-target and missing-target hardlink tests still reject.

**Observed result after fix:** `result.ok === true` for the legacy internal-hardlink archive; traversal/absolute/missing targets still throw.

**What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check && pnpm test`; the reporter's actual archive; the `backup create --verify` exit-13 temp-archive failure; applying to `release/2026.5.28`.
